title: Ubuntu packages missing dependency package "uidmap"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After installation it is missing the binary "newuidmap"

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install a fresh copy of Ubuntu 20.04 (from Armbian) on a Rock64 SoC

2. add the apt repository as instructed on https://podman.io/getting-started/installation which results in this deb line in the sources: 
```
deb http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/ /
```
and then 
```
apt update && apt install podman
```
it will install with no errors and also run as root but will refuse to run rootless as a normal user:
```
Error: cannot find newuidmap: exec: "newuidmap": executable file not found in $PATH
```

The problem can be easily fixed if the missing package is installed. On Ubuntu the missing package is ```uidmap``` and it is available in the official Ubuntu repositories. This package should be added to the dependencies of the .deb package so it will make sure it is pulled in during apt-get on such installations where it does not seem to be installed by default.

**Describe the results you expected:**
should have pulled all needed dependencies

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
bernd@rock:~$ apt list podman
Auflistung... Fertig
podman/unbekannt 100:3.0.1-2 amd64
podman/unbekannt,now 100:3.0.1-2 arm64  [installiert]
podman/unbekannt 100:3.0.1-2 armhf
podman/unbekannt 100:3.0.1-2 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
It is "recommended" but not required, since it is not needed when running `sudo podman`

Depends: libseccomp2 (>= 2.4.3-1), libdevmapper1.02.1, libgpgme11, catatonit, conmon (>= 100:2.0.25~2), containers-common (>= 100:1-12), dbus-user-session, iptables, podman-plugins (>= 100:1.1.1-5), crun (>= 100:0.17-4)
Recommends: slirp4netns (>= 100:1.1.8-3), containernetworking-plugins (>= 100:0.9.1-1), **uidmap**, fuse-overlayfs
Yes this is fine,  it is a recommends since it is not required for running podman as root.
